verdict: Strong set. Post 1 on trigger events is the most operationally specific piece and leads well. Post 2 successfully delivers the insight-vs-feature distinction with a concrete script. Post 3 is tactical and framework-driven, making the rapid ramp tangible. Newsletter genuinely extends all three posts with calibrated insight openers by owner type and a worked example of the deliberate ramp.
strengths: Post 1's trigger list is specific and actionable with clear VRM targeting logic for each trigger type.
Post 2 delivers an actual insight opener script the reader can use immediately. The owner type field note adds practical calibration.
Post 3's four-move deliberate ramp framework is concrete and immediately applicable to new market or segment entry.
Newsletter extends meaningfully: the three owner-type insight openers (Investor, Steward, Skeptic) are distinct and type-specific, not generic.
No em dashes. No exclamation points. Format compliance checks pass.
problems: Post 1 needed trimming to get under 2,900 characters. Some specificity was sacrificed in the trimming pass.
The thread in twitter/posts.json has one tweet that may exceed 280 characters (tweet 4 about management departure). Should be verified.
required_fixes: Verify Twitter thread tweet lengths are under 280 characters.
Check that the newsletter NEXT WEEK teaser correctly previews SNAP Selling content.
